scope.summary: Constitution rule 17 plus `swarm_discipline.sh struct-atomicity`: a change that adds a struct field is checked against the literal and exhaustive-pattern sites it leaves untouched, which are exactly the sites that stop compiling when the field lands.
out_of_scope: The four earlier incidents in the same session, carried by the sibling beads under oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.
The rust-oracledb half: its AGENTS.md still carries the twelve-rule constitution and none of these mechanisms.
The general case of rule 17 (a git mv and its references, a trait method and its impls): stated in the charter, mechanized only for the struct-field case that broke this session's build.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh struct-atomicity
entry_points.trace: `--selftest` builds a two-file crate, stages a field added to `Ctx` alone and gets exit 65 naming crates/demo/src/other.rs, then stages the matching initializer and gets exit 0. Against real history it is quiet where it should be: --commit HEAD~1 (15 added fields) and --commit HEAD~2 (11 added fields) both report no orphaned initializer sites.

limitations: Static, not a compiler: it finds struct-literal and struct-pattern sites by name and skips any block using `..`, so a field added behind #[serde(default)] or a builder that never names the type is out of its reach.
It reads a staged diff or one commit; it does not span a series of commits.
